content:
r-near opened PR #12938 from r-near:winch-nan-canonicalization to bytecodealliance:main:

The enable_nan_canonicalization flag already flows through to Winch via the shared Flags, but Winch was ignoring it. This adds a canonicalize_nan method to the Masm trait that, when the flag is set, emits a compare-with-self + conditional branch to replace NaN results with the canonical quiet NaN after each float arithmetic op.

Covered operations: add, sub, mul, div, min, max, sqrt, ceil, floor, trunc, nearest, demote, and promote. Implemented for x64 and aarch64. Includes a scalar wast test (counterpart to simd/canonicalize-nan.wast).

Copilot created PR review comment:

The sum < MAX_READ_SIZE_ALLOC loop condition allows sum to exceed the limit because sum is incremented after receiving a datagram. If sum is just below the limit, one more large datagram can push sum far past the cap. To enforce the cap, compute the prospective increment and break/return before pushing when it would exceed the limit.

Copilot submitted PR review:

Pull request overview

This PR’s stated goal is to make Winch respect enable_nan_canonicalization, but the diff as provided spans a much broader set of changes across Winch, Wasmtime runtime limits, WASI/http behaviors, pooling allocator/coredump behavior, CI/release automation, and a workspace-wide version bump.

Changes:

  • Add MacroAssembler::canonicalize_nan and invoke it after scalar float ops (and rounding builtins) in Winch; implement for x64 and aarch64; add a scalar NaN-canonicalization wast test.
  • Introduce/propagate several host-side resource limiting knobs (hostcall fuel, resource table capacity, random len cap, HTTP fields size cap) and apply allocation caps in various WASI/WASI-http codepaths.
  • Update release/versioning and publishing/release automation (36.0.6 / Cranelift 0.123.6, supply-chain audit config changes, GitHub release workflow tweaks).

Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 97 out of 109 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments.

Copilot created PR review comment:

write_zeroes allocates a buffer of size nelem (Bytes::from_iter(..take(nelem))). Since check_write() can legitimately return very large values (e.g. usize::MAX for a sink stream), a guest can trigger an attempted huge host allocation here. Consider writing zeroes in bounded chunks (e.g. min(nelem, permit, fixed max)) or using a small static zero-buffer loop rather than allocating nelem bytes at once.

Copilot created PR review comment:

Using unwrap_or(u32::MAX) as a sentinel for shared memories means instance memory indices in the core dump can point outside the serialized memories section. This risks producing an invalid core dump. Instead, consider filtering shared memories out of instance.all_memories(..) here (to match store_memories.retain(..)) or otherwise using a spec-defined encoding for "no memory" if one exists.

Copilot created PR review comment:

octokit.rest.repos.updateRelease(...) is not awaited. Since runOnce is async and the action may exit immediately after runOnce returns, the release can remain a draft intermittently. Please await this call (and consider error handling/retry like the other API calls).

    try {
      await octokit.rest.repos.updateRelease({
          owner,
          repo,
          release_id: release.data.id,
          draft: false,
      });
    } catch (e) {
      console.log("ERROR: ", JSON.stringify(e, null, 2));
      core.info(`failed to update release draft status`);
      throw e;
    }

Copilot created PR review comment:

This PR is titled/described as a Winch NaN-canonicalization change, but the diff includes many unrelated changes (WASI limits/hostcall fuel, coredump/shared memory handling, supply-chain audits, release/version bumps, CI/release workflow tweaks, etc.). Please either update the PR description to cover the full scope or split these into focused PRs to make review and risk assessment tractable.

Copilot created PR review comment:

Doc comment formatting: "See [Store::set_hostcall_fuel] for more details." has an unmatched backtick and reads oddly. Consider changing to "See [Store::set_hostcall_fuel`] for more details."

Copilot created PR review comment:

The docs say "The default value for this is 128 MiB" but DEFAULT_HOSTCALL_FUEL is 2 << 30 (~2 GiB). Please make the docs and the actual default consistent (either adjust the constant or update the documented default).
